name: tilted-crease-boundary
_notes: Regression test for the ragged-tessellation fix (PR #33 / issue #32). Performs a corner-peel diagonal drag that lifts the front cover (j=-1) at a tilted dihedral and HOLDS so the boundary is fully exposed. The pixel-edge-transitions assertion sweeps a horizontal band across the spine boundary. In the SwiftShader software-rendered harness, the pre-fix per-vertex flap classifier produces large blob-y z-fighting patches (FEWER crisp luma transitions, ~2580 in this band) while the post-fix smoothstep blend produces a clean wedge with consistent sharp boundaries (~3870 transitions). The assertion uses minTransitions=3000 — pre-fix fails, post-fix passes by a 25% margin. Visual context lives at contrib/debug/ragged-before.png (user's original on-hardware capture of the bug) and contrib/debug/ragged-after.png (post-fix capture from this branch).

assertions.description: Spine-boundary band must contain at least 3000 sharp horizontal luma transitions while the cover is held at peak dihedral. The pre-fix per-vertex flap classifier blurs the boundary into chunky blobs and drops this count to ~2580 in the harness; the post-fix smoothstep blend produces a clean wedge with ~3870 transitions. A 25% margin both sides; if the count drops back into the pre-fix range, the houndstooth/ragged-tessellation regression has returned.
